criteriaResults.reasoning: The condition in sort.py now reads 'if numbers[i] > numbers[j]' instead of the original 'if numbers[i] < numbers[j]'.
With the corrected > operator, the bubble sort algorithm will sort numbers in ascending order, producing [1, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9].
